title: Mespilus biltmoreana
intro: Mespilus biltmoreana, also known as the Biltmore mayhaw, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Mespilus biltmoreana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mespilus biltmoreana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Mespilus biltmoreana is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Rosaceae. This family is commonly known as the rose family and includes various other species of plants such as roses, apples, and plums. The genus Mespilus is a small group of shrubs and small trees native to North America and Europe, with Mespilus biltmoreana being a specific species found in the southeastern United States. The plant is often referred to as the Biltmore mayhaw or the Biltmore haw, and is known for its edible fruit. Mespilus biltmoreana is often classified as a member of the Rosoideae subfamily within the Rosaceae family.
